textPlain: Sounds like this framework is comparable in size to the original Rails, which clocked in at under 1000 lines of code (microdot is 765 lines per the article).I don't know if the original Rails would have run on mruby though (if it had existed at the time), and Rails certainly did a lot of things the author of microdot would have considered "dark magic".

Microdot is apparently a Python web framework that runs on both CPython and MicroPython.It is a single 765-line file with routing, JSON handling, cookies, streaming, and TLS. Created to provide a web server for IoT devices.
